title: Compound Annual Growth Rate
definition: The geometric progression ratio that provides a constant rate of return over a specified time period. Formula: [(End Value / Start Value)^(1 / Years)] - 1.

title: Price-to-Earnings Ratio
definition: The valuation metric calculated by dividing the company's current share market price by its Earnings Per Share (EPS). Used to value companies.

title: Exchange Traded Fund
definition: A type of pooled investment security that operates much like a mutual fund but is bought and sold directly on stock exchanges throughout the day.

title: Systematic Investment Plan
definition: An investment methodology where a fixed amount of capital is regularly channeled into mutual funds or index funds, enabling rupee-cost averaging.

title: Asset Allocation Plan
definition: The strategy of dividing an investment portfolio among different asset classes, such as stocks (equity), bonds (debt), gold, and cash, to manage volatility.

title: Financial Independence, Retire Early
definition: A lifestyle movement aimed at extreme savings and investing (often 50-70% of income) to retire decades earlier than traditional age limits.

title: Employer-Sponsored Retirement Account
definition: A retirement savings plan sponsored by an employer. It lets workers save and invest a piece of their paycheck before taxes are taken out.

title: Annual Percentage Rate
definition: The yearly interest rate charged on borrowing or earned on investing, without taking compounding into account.

title: Annual Percentage Yield
definition: The real rate of return earned on an investment, taking into account the effect of compounding interest.

title: Amortization Schedule
definition: The process of spreading out a loan into a series of fixed periodic payments over time, where each payment covers both principal and interest.

title: Asset Valuation
definition: A resource with economic value that an individual, corporation, or country owns or controls with the expectation that it will provide a future benefit.

title: Bear Market Cycle
definition: A condition in which securities prices fall 20% or more from recent highs, accompanied by widespread pessimism and negative investor sentiment.

title: Volatility Beta Coefficient
definition: A measure of a stock's volatility or systematic risk in comparison to the entire market. A beta of 1.0 matches the market.

title: Debt Instrument Bond
definition: A fixed-income instrument that represents a loan made by an investor to a borrower (typically corporate or governmental).

title: Bull Market Trend
definition: A financial market of a group of securities in which prices are rising or are expected to rise over a sustained period.

title: Capital Gains Tax
definition: An increase in the value of a capital asset (investment or real estate) that gives it a higher worth than the purchase price.

title: Net Cash Flow
definition: The net amount of cash and cash-equivalents being transferred into and out of a business or personal account.

title: Power of Compounding
definition: The addition of interest to the principal sum of a loan or deposit, or interest on interest. Formula: A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt).

title: FICO Credit Rating
definition: A numerical expression based on a level analysis of a person's credit files, to represent the creditworthiness of an individual.

title: DTI Ratio
definition: The percentage of a consumer's monthly gross income that goes toward paying debts (such as loans, mortgages, or credit cards).

title: Portfolio Diversification
definition: A risk management strategy that mixes a wide variety of investments within a portfolio to limit exposure to any single asset.

title: Dividend Yield Payout
definition: The distribution of some of a company's earnings to a class of its shareholders, determined by the company's board of directors.

title: Liquid Cash Shield
definition: An easily accessible stash of cash set aside to cover financial surprises or job losses, preventing forced debt or portfolio liquidations.

title: Purchasing Power Inflation
definition: The rate at which the general level of prices for goods and services is rising, and, consequently, purchasing power is falling.

title: Pooled Investment Fund
definition: A company that pools money from many investors and invests the money in securities such as stocks, bonds, and short-term debt.

title: Net Assets Valuation
definition: The value of all the non-financial and financial assets owned by an individual or institution, minus all its outstanding liabilities.

title: Investment Portfolio
definition: A grouping of financial assets such as stocks, bonds, commodities, currencies, and cash equivalents, as well as their mutual fund counterparts.

title: Original Loan Principal
definition: The original sum of money lent or invested on a compound interest rate, separate from the interest that accumulates over time.

title: Risk Capacity Quiz
definition: The degree of variability in investment returns that an investor is willing to withstand in their financial planning.

title: Market Volatility Index
definition: A statistical measure of the dispersion of returns for a given security or market index, representing rate variations.
